text.title: JS/TS Frontend Testing Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the testing-js-ts-frontend methodology. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Tests MUST query elements by getByRole > getByLabelText > getByText > getByDisplayValue. getByTestId is allowed only when no semantic option exists, with an inline comment explaining why. CSS-class queries are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Accessible-attribute queries test what users see; CSS-class queries test implementation and break silently on style refactors.

rule.statement: Vitest projects MUST either set globals: true in vitest.config.ts OR import { describe, it, expect, vi } explicitly. Mixing jest globals into a vitest project is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Mixed globals cause TypeScript errors when @types/jest is absent or silent pollution when present; explicit imports make the runner visible.

rule.statement: Mock APIs MUST match the runner: vi.fn() / vi.mock() in Vitest projects; jest.fn() / jest.mock() in Jest projects. Cross-API usage is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Cross-API calls fail at import or silently behave differently; tests become flaky.

rule.statement: Snapshot tests MUST NOT be the default strategy for components. They are allowed only with an explicit justification + ≤30-line snapshot bodies.
rule.rationale: Large snapshots get rubber-stamped on update; reviewable diffs fall through. Targeted assertions catch real regressions.

rule.statement: Interactions (click, type, hover) MUST go through userEvent (not fireEvent). userEvent simulates the full event sequence (focus, keydown, keyup, click).
rule.rationale: fireEvent dispatches a single synthetic event; misses the realistic sequence that catches real-world bugs.

rule.statement: Every component test file MUST include one axe-core or jest-axe assertion against the rendered output. Bolted-on a11y later is forbidden.
rule.rationale: axe surfaces a11y regressions automatically; cheap when added per component, hard to retrofit on hundreds of files.
